summary: Pulls in generically-named unpinned transitive dependency 'node-core-libs'
details: node-fetch-utils 1.2.8 declares a runtime dependency on 'node-core-libs@^1.0.0' (package.json line 24), a generically-named package that does not correspond to any well-known npm library and is unpinned to a caret range. The package's own code (index.js, lib/retry.js, lib/cache.js) is benign HTTP fetch utility code with no lifecycle hooks, no outbound exfiltration, and no obfuscation. However, installing this package will transitively pull in node-core-libs, whose code has not been evaluated here and which carries the typical supply-chain risks of an unpinned, generically-named dependency (dependency confusion, hidden payload in the transitive). Routing to human review so the transitive can be evaluated independently before this package is trusted in a build pipeline.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
